activities_projects.title: Constituency Election Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the process of winning a seat and forming a majority
steps: Using a list of 70 seats, distribute them among four different parties
Identify the 'Magic Number' (half the total seats + 1) required to form a government
Determine if the leading party can form the government alone or needs a 'Coalition'
Label the parties as 'Ruling Party' and 'Opposition' based on the numbers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes how numerical strength determines who holds power in the Legislative Assembly
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in calculating the majority and identifying the ruling/opposition parties (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Legislative Assembly Debate Script
activities_projects.objective: Simulate the process of questioning and accountability
materials: Role cards (MLA of Ruling Party, MLA of Opposition, Minister of Health)
steps: Draft a 2-minute speech for an Opposition MLA criticizing the lack of sanitation in their district
Draft a 2-minute response for the Health Minister defending the government's actions
Include a section where the 'Speaker' intervenes to maintain order
Discuss why the Minister is personally responsible for the department's failures
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates the importance of debate and ministerial responsibility in a democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and understanding of the accountability mechanism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Governor’s Appointment Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the link between State and Central governments
steps: Research how a Governor is appointed (not elected) by the Central Government
List two specific duties the Governor performs (e.g., inviting the CM to form government, signing bills)
Discuss why the Governor is called the 'Head of the State' while the CM is 'Head of the Government'
Find the name of the current Governor of your state and their background
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes between the constitutional head and the executive head of a state
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in explaining the Governor's role and appointment process (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Press Conference vs. Assembly Debate
activities_projects.objective: Compare different ways the government answers for its actions
steps: Identify who asks questions in a Press Conference (Journalists) vs. the Assembly (MLAs)
Compare the goals: Informing the general public vs. passing laws/budgets
Analyze why a government must do both to be considered transparent
Create a Venn Diagram showing the similarities and differences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the roles of the legislature and the media in a functioning democracy
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison in the Venn Diagram (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Government Departments Flowchart
activities_projects.objective: Trace the hierarchy of executive power
steps: Place 'Chief Minister' at the top of the chart
Branch out into different 'Cabinet Ministers' (Education, Health, Agriculture)
Below each Minister, show the 'Secretaries' (IAS officers) and departments
Explain the path of an order from the CM’s office down to a local district
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the administrative structure of the State Executive
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical flow and correct naming of hierarchy levels (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coalition Government Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Explore the challenges of multi-party governance
steps: Scenario: No single party has reached the 50% mark for majority
Negotiate between Party A (40% seats) and Party C (12% seats) to reach a majority
List two challenges a CM might face when running a coalition (e.g., disagreement on policies)
Write a brief 'Common Minimum Program' that both parties agree on
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understand how power-sharing works when there is no clear majority
activities_projects.evaluation: Realism of the negotiation points and the shared program (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Wall Newspaper: The Patalpuram Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how information is presented to the public
steps: Focus on the Patalpuram story in the book
Collect news snippets about health or water issues in your own area
Summarize what the 'People' said, what the 'Opposition' said, and what the 'Government' did
Present it as a 'Wall Newspaper' similar to the one shown in the NCERT text
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes different perspectives on a single public issue
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in representing multiple viewpoints (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Speaker’s Rulebook
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of neutrality in the Assembly
steps: Research how the 'Speaker' of the Legislative Assembly is chosen
List three powers the Speaker has during a session
Explain why the Speaker usually does not vote unless there is a tie
Design a 'Code of Conduct' poster for MLAs during a session
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the role of the Speaker in ensuring democratic procedure
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the Speaker's neutral and authoritative role (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Local Issue Action Plan
activities_projects.objective: Apply the steps of state governance to a real problem
steps: Pick a local issue (e.g., broken roads or lack of streetlights)
Identify which State Government department is responsible for it
Write a letter to your local MLA describing the problem
Draft a short 'Press Release' the department might issue once the work is done
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates textbook concepts of governance to active citizenship
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the responsible department and formal tone of the letter (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Defining 'The Government' - A Concept Map
activities_projects.objective: Clarify that 'Government' includes both Ministers and Departments
steps: Create a concept map with 'The Government' in the center
Connect it to 'Legislative Assembly' (MLAs who deliberate)
Connect it to 'Executive' (Ministers and officials who implement)
Briefly explain how the Executive is chosen from the Legislature
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Clarifies the dual meaning of 'Government' as used in common language and political science
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the concept map and correctness of the relationships (10 marks)
